description:
You are working as an integration developer and need a very quick sanity-check that the *local* mock API response you have just produced can be retrieved with curl.

    1. A JSON file already exists at  
       /home/user/mock/api/greeting.json  

       Exact contents (single line, no leading or trailing spaces or newline):  
       {"message":"Hello, integration test!"}

    2. With a **single** terminal command that uses **curl**, fetch that file through the file-URL scheme and write the raw response **unchanged** into  
       /home/user/api_test/curl_response.log

       • If the directory /home/user/api_test does not yet exist, create it within the same command (i.e. do not run an extra command).  
       • The resulting log file must contain *exactly* the same single-line JSON shown above, with **no extra line-breaks or whitespace** at either the beginning or end of the file.  
       • The automated tests will look for the presence of the file and will compare its contents byte-for-byte against the expected string.

    3. Do not perform any additional steps; the entire task must be satisfied by one shell command.
truth:
<!-- File that is present before the task starts -->
    <pre_existing_file path="/home/user/mock/api/greeting.json">
        {"message":"Hello, integration test!"}
    </pre_existing_file>

    <!-- File that must be created by the agent -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/api_test/curl_response.log">
        {"message":"Hello, integration test!"}
    </expected_file>
